title: Summarize the whole log
prompt: Write summarize(entries) returning a dict: 'total' (count), 'by_level' (the level->count dict), 'first' and 'last' (the timestamp of the first and last entry, or None if empty).

details.restate: Boil the whole entry list down to one summary dictionary with four keys: 'total' (how many entries), 'by_level' (the level-to-count dictionary), 'first' and 'last' (the timestamp of the first and last entry — or None for both if the list is empty).

io.call: summarize([t1 INFO, t2 ERROR, t3 INFO])
io.because: count, per-level tally, and the two bookend timestamps
an empty log still summarizes cleanly
